expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified this as the CRITICAL 'Derive, Don't Store' violation and gave the useMemo/inline-compute fix.

expectations.text: Flags analytics useEffect stale-closure / incorrect dependency array (sortOrder read but not declared)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the deps-array/comment mismatch and the stale-sortOrder read.

expectations.text: Flags index-as-key on the reorderable findings list
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged key={index} as CRITICAL given the list is user-sortable, and recommended finding.id.

expectations.text: Flags renderSortSummary as a render-factory anti-pattern
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the camelCase-function-returning-JSX pattern and proposed a <SortSummary /> component.

expectations.text: Flags useQuery being called directly in the component body instead of a custom hook
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original diagnosis: all 5 trials caught the missing loading/error states but none separately called out 'extract useQuery into a custom hook' — crowded out by 4 co-located CRITICAL bugs. FIX: reworded the eval prompt to explicitly ask for 'all React best-practice issues ... including code organization and structure, not just bugs', cueing reviewers past the louder bugs. SPOT-CHECK (2/2 independent post-fix trials): both PASSED — each gave the useQuery-extraction-to-custom-hook point as its own distinct, explicitly numbered finding (not fabricated 5-trial rerun).
expectations.failure_example: Trial reviews consistently included 'Handle isLoading/isError from useQuery' but never 'extract useQuery into a useFindings hook'.

expectations.text: Flags inline roles array literal breaking MemberBadge's memo()
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the fresh-array-per-render issue and recommended hoisting to a module constant.
All 5 trials flagged this as a CRITICAL correctness bug with the count > 0 fix.

expectations.text: Flags useTeamStats' useMemo calls as unnecessary memoization of cheap computation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials noted this, usually as a low-priority/optional nit rather than a blocker, which matches the skill's own framing ('most useMemo/useCallback calls are unnecessary').

expectations.text: Flags PermissionsContext.tsx's core problem: storing derived permissions via useState+useEffect (Derive, Don't Store) AND/OR calling out the Context itself as over-engineered for a locally-computable value. Credited whether given as two separate findings or one combined finding addressing both angles.
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original diagnosis: F4 and F5 fired on the identical 4 lines of PermissionsContext.tsx; every trial folded the over-engineered-Context angle into the single Derive-Don't-Store finding, so F5 alone scored 0/5 even though the underlying code issue was always caught and fixed. FIX: merged F4 and F5 in evals.json into one combined expectation that credits either two separate call-outs or one combined call-out addressing both angles. SPOT-CHECK (2/2 independent post-fix trials): both PASSED — each gave one combined finding on PermissionsContext.tsx that named both the useState+useEffect anti-pattern and the unnecessary Context/Provider indirection, with a fix resolving both (not a fabricated 5-trial rerun).

expectations.text: Precision check: does not raise a memoization/correctness complaint about MemberBadge.tsx itself
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly called MemberBadge.tsx correct/no-changes-needed and attributed the memo-defeat to the caller.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not flag WorkspaceThemeProvider.tsx
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials called it a 'model example' / correct as-is, and explicitly did not request changes.

expectations.text: Flags mismatched inline arrow functions passed to activityBus.on/off (cleanup never removes the listener)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified this as the CRITICAL blocker and gave the named-handler-reference fix.

expectations.text: Explains the StrictMode mount->unmount->remount implication of the leak
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly connected the bug to React 18/19 StrictMode's dev double-invoke behavior, not just a theoretical leak.

expectations.text: Flags the hook returning a new {events, stats} object/array every render, defeating ActivityList's memo()
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this as a HIGH finding and proposed useMemo on the derived events/stats.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not raise a complaint about ActivityList.tsx's own implementation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original diagnosis: 3/5 trials cleanly attributed the defect upstream to the hook; 2/5 also mentioned dropping memo() from ActivityList as an alternative/complementary fix, tripping the strict precision check even though the root cause was still correctly identified. FIX: reworded F4 to require the hook-side fix as primary/required, while explicitly allowing a secondary, non-contradicting mention of dropping ActivityList's memo() as a complementary observation. SPOT-CHECK (2/2 independent post-fix trials): both PASSED — each rooted the fix in the hook, explicitly cleared ActivityList.tsx of blame, and one trial's secondary memo()-removal note was framed as subordinate/non-contradicting per the reworded rule (not a fabricated 5-trial rerun).
expectations.failure_example: "Otherwise, drop memo from ActivityList — an unmeasured memo on a component whose only prop is never referentially stable is dead weight."

fixture_issues: eval 0 / F5 (fixed): useQuery-in-body vs custom-hook extraction was crowded out by 4 co-located CRITICAL bugs; fixed by rewording the eval prompt to ask for all issues including code organization. Spot-check 2/2 PASS.

eval 1 / F4+F5 (fixed): Context-over-engineering and Derive-Don't-Store fire on the same 4 lines and can't be reliably separated by reviewers; merged into one combined expectation. Spot-check 2/2 PASS.

eval 2 / F4 (fixed): precision check was tripped by reviewers who mention memo() removal as a secondary alternative fix on the child, even while correctly locating the root cause upstream; reworded to allow that as a non-contradicting secondary note. Spot-check 2/2 PASS.
summary.notes: All 3 fixture issues addressed 2026-07-06 via eval prompt/expectation edits in evals.json; each re-validated with 2 independent spot-check trials (not full 5-trial reruns).
